Question type: short-answer. Papers provided. The three required judgments are closed-book solvable because the stem asks the solver to evaluate claims against the described evidence, and the relevant thresholds/mechanisms are explicitly present in the evidence bundle: (i) gas onset near 70–80°C for all VC concentrations/formation rates (C002), which directly contradicts the engineer’s “higher onset temperature” safety-margin claim; (ii) ~3× higher POF3 specifically for 0.4 vol% VC with C/10 formation (C003), supporting the reviewer’s fluorine-gas concern and requiring the combination condition; (iii) ~130°C residual-VC-driven EC/VC cross-polymerization under strong CO2 evolution leading to rapid pressure rise/venting (C005), supporting the second concern. No internal ambiguity is evident because each claim maps to a specific cited threshold/mechanism, and no Correct-labeled/gold claims are contradicted by the evidence.
